articles.expand: The Pound Sterling (GBP) rallies on Wednesday, advancing for the third straight day this week, up more than 1.10% due to broad US Dollar (USD) weakness, sparked by an improvement in risk appetite following a two-week ceasefire between the US and Iran.

articles.title: Reserve Bank of New Zealand Holds OCR at 2.25%
articles.expand: The RBNZ held its OCR at 2.25% for a second straight meeting, but a hawkish tone from Governor Breman and a US-Iran ceasefire sent the Kiwi surging.

articles.title: WTI slides sharply after US-Iran ceasefire removes war premium
articles.expand: West Texas Intermediate (WTI) Crude Oil trades sharply lower on Wednesday, falling more than 10% after a temporary ceasefire agreement between the United States and Iran reduced the geopolitical risk premium embedded in prices.

articles.title: The Iran war may be over, inflation is definitely not
articles.expand: Euphoria. That’s the best way to describe the market reaction to Tuesday's headlines about the US-Iran two-week ceasefire.

articles.title: Brent: Elevated prices after ceasefire relief – Commerzbank
articles.expand: Commerzbank’s Thu Lan Nguyen notes that Brent prices dropped by USD 15 on news of a two‑week U.S.–Iran ceasefire and reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, but still trade near USD 95, well above pre‑war levels.

articles.title: Oil tankers passing through Hormuz stopped after Israel's ceasefire breach – Fars News Agency
articles.expand: Iran's Fars News Agency reported on Wednesday that oil tankers passing through the Strait of Hormuz have been stopped after Israel breached the ceasefire, per Reuters.

articles.title: Gold (XAU/USD) stalls at critical $4900/oz resistance as bear signal flashes
articles.expand: Gold's "war premium" is fading after the US-Iran ceasefire sparked a rally, now stalled near $4900 resistance. The metal faces binary risks: a truce failure could send it to $4500, while strong net-long sentiment suggests a near-term slide. Dollar softness and silver's 7% rally provide underlying support.

articles.title: Canada: Tariff shock lessons shape policy – RBC
articles.expand: Royal Bank of Canada (RBC) analysts review how Canada has weathered one year of U.S. tariff shocks. They note that Canada’s Gross Domestic Product (GDP) and unemployment held up, but sectoral and regional damage was significant.

articles.title: New playbook for India's bank boards: RBI Guv Sanjay Malhotra reveals plan for revision of guidelines
articles.expand: The Reserve Bank of India is set to update bank board guidelines with an aim to shift board focus from daily operations to policy matters. Central bank Governor Sanjay Malhotra announced the plan at his policy meet address. The move, which follows recent issues at HDFC Bank, will allow boards to dedicate more time to strategic policy decisions.
